page: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the EASv4 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, configured with 4 physical cores and 8 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) via hyper-threading. The system features dedicated CPU allocation and includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 524 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, while containing zero GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics generally fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average read-only performance for both small and large blocks, though large-block write memory bandwidth is weak, scoring in the bottom 25% at 23.0 GB/sec. Large-model LLM prompt processing is also a relative bottleneck, with the 7B model prompt processing benchmark registering in the bottom 25% at 16.95 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E32-8as_v4 offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The server is well-suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and memory-heavy enterprise applications where a 32 GB RAM per vCPU ratio is advantageous.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 524 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, though its multi-core and memory read metrics remain average. A notable performance tradeoff is its weak large-block write memory bandwidth and slower prompt processing for medium-sized LLM models. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems that require substantial memory capacity without requiring high-density multi-core compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, designed for memory-heavy database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high memory density, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and local storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core
524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and enterprise applications
